From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Faith Theological Seminary](/w/index.php?title=Faith_Theological_Seminary&redirect=no "Faith Theological Seminary"))

Christian seminary in Baltimore, Maryland, USA

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Faith Theological Seminary, Baltimore"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Faith+Theological+Seminary%2C+Baltimore%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Faith+Theological+Seminary%2C+Baltimore%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Faith+Theological+Seminary%2C+Baltimore%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Faith+Theological+Seminary%2C+Baltimore%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Faith+Theological+Seminary%2C+Baltimore%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Faith+Theological+Seminary%2C+Baltimore%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(September 2024)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

Faith Theological Seminary

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Type | [Private](/wiki/Private_university "Private university") [unaccredited](/wiki/Higher_education_accreditation_in_the_United_States "Higher education accreditation in the United States") [seminary](/wiki/Seminary "Seminary") |
| Established | 1937; 89 years ago (1937) |
| Religious affiliation | [Evangelicalism](/wiki/Evangelicalism "Evangelicalism") |
| Location | [Baltimore](/wiki/Baltimore,_Maryland "Baltimore, Maryland") , [Maryland](/wiki/Maryland "Maryland") , United States |
| Website | [ftscatonsville.org/](https://ftscatonsville.org/) |
|  | |
| Map | |

**Faith Theological Seminary** is an [unaccredited](/wiki/Higher_education_accreditation_in_the_United_States "Higher education accreditation in the United States") [evangelical Christian](/wiki/Evangelicalism "Evangelicalism") seminary in [Baltimore, Maryland](/wiki/Baltimore,_Maryland "Baltimore, Maryland"). It was founded in 1937[[1]](#cite_note-History-1) in [Wilmington, Delaware](/wiki/Wilmington,_Delaware "Wilmington, Delaware"), relocated to Philadelphia in 1952, and then moved to Maryland in 2004.[[1]](#cite_note-History-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Faith_Theological_Seminary,_Baltimore&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

In response to the Presbyterian controversies of the early twentieth century, in 1929, under the leadership of J. Gresham Machen, a group of scholars was organized to start a new institution. They found quarters for the new seminary (Westminster Theological Seminary) in two townhouses in Philadelphia, housing students in the Drake Hotel.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Allan A. MacRae served as the first president of FTS from 1937 until 1971.[[1]](#cite_note-History-1)[[2]](#cite_note-FPCHistory-2)[[3]](#cite_note-Harden-3) [*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Subsequent to McIntire's death, Norman J. Manohar assumed the presidency, moving the seminary to Maryland, until September 14, 2020 when he was terminated by the board of directors for "gross financial mismanagement, misappropriation, and negligence" and for having fraudulently represented his degrees to accreditors (Maryland Higher Education Commission [MHEC] Investigative Report of Sept 9, 2019, states that claiming unearned credentials is "incongruent with the 'demonstrated history of ethical practice required by COMAR 13B.02.02.13B'"). A private attorney's Investigative Report of January 14, 2019, identified the misrepresentation of several academic credentials and financial malfeasance. Further, an investigative report conducted by the institution's accrediting body, the Transnational Association of Christian Schools and Colleges (TRACS) led to termination of accreditation on May 21, 2020 (TRACS correspondence, May 29, 2020). Pursuant to the regulations of COMAR I 3B.02.02.08L(6), MHEC subsequently removed its Authorization as of May 21, 2020 (MHEC correspondence, Secretary of Academic Affairs, June 15, 2020).[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Academics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Faith_Theological_Seminary,_Baltimore&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Academics")]

Faith Theological Seminary previously offered a [Bachelor of Arts](/wiki/Bachelor_of_Arts "Bachelor of Arts") (BA) in Religion, a [Master of Divinity](/wiki/Master_of_Divinity "Master of Divinity") (M.Div.), a [Doctor of Ministry](/wiki/Doctor_of_Ministry "Doctor of Ministry") (D.Min.), and a [Doctor of Theology](/wiki/Doctor_of_Theology "Doctor of Theology") (Th.D.) degree. The institution was accredited by the [Transnational Association of Christian Colleges and Schools](/wiki/Transnational_Association_of_Christian_Colleges_and_Schools "Transnational Association of Christian Colleges and Schools") (TRACS) but lost accreditation in May 2020. When Faith Theological Seminary lost its accreditation from TRACS in May 2020, the state of Maryland also subsequently suspended its degree-granting privileges. It was closed with a [teach-out](/wiki/Teach-out "Teach-out") plan implemented with Lancaster Bible College and Capital Seminary. In 2021, Faith Theological Seminary of Catonsville reincorporated and received a religious exemption approval to operate in the state of Maryland and grant undergraduate and graduate degrees.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Notable alumni

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Faith_Theological_Seminary,_Baltimore&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Notable alumni")]

* [Arthur Glasser](/wiki/Arthur_Glasser "Arthur Glasser"), 1942[[4]](#cite_note-NotableAlumni-4)
* [Vernon Grounds](/wiki/Vernon_Grounds "Vernon Grounds"), 1940[[5]](#cite_note-Grounds-5)[[4]](#cite_note-NotableAlumni-4)
* [Kenneth Kantzer](/wiki/Kenneth_Kantzer "Kenneth Kantzer"), 1942[[6]](#cite_note-Kantzer-6)[[4]](#cite_note-NotableAlumni-4)
* [Francis Schaeffer](/wiki/Francis_Schaeffer "Francis Schaeffer"), 1938[[7]](#cite_note-MacRae-7)[[8]](#cite_note-FrancisSchaeffer-8)[[4]](#cite_note-NotableAlumni-4)
* [Arthur E. Steele](/wiki/Arthur_E._Steele "Arthur E. Steele"), 1959[[4]](#cite_note-NotableAlumni-4)
* [Timothy Tow](/wiki/Timothy_Tow "Timothy Tow")[[9]](#cite_note-9)
